primary_meaning: a person's view or judgment about something
secondary_meaning: what someone thinks rather than knows
usage_notes: Use with 'in my opinion', 'have an opinion', 'share opinions'
common_collocations: in my opinion, public opinion, strong opinion, opinion about
image_search_query: person sharing viewpoint or speech bubble with opinion
image_context: A person gesturing while sharing their viewpoint, or thought bubbles expressing different opinions
example_1: In my opinion, this is the best restaurant in town.
example_1_translation: Na minha opinião, este é o melhor restaurante da cidade.
example_2: She has strong opinions about politics.
example_2_translation: Ela tem opiniões fortes sobre política.
example_3: Public opinion has changed on this issue.
example_3_translation: A opinião pública mudou sobre este assunto.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: o-PIN-ion. Emphasize second: o-PIN-ion
memory_hook: Op-in-ion - express your 'pin' (point) of view

primary_meaning: the way you think and feel about someone or something
secondary_meaning: your behavior toward someone or something
usage_notes: Use with 'have an attitude', 'positive attitude', 'attitude toward'
common_collocations: positive attitude, negative attitude, attitude toward, have an attitude
image_search_query: person with confident posture or thinking gesture
image_context: A person standing with confident body language or a mind map showing different attitudes
example_1: She has a very positive attitude toward her work.
example_1_translation: Ela tem uma atitude muito positiva em relação ao seu trabalho.
example_2: His attitude needs to change if he wants to succeed.
example_2_translation: Sua atitude precisa mudar se ele quiser ter sucesso.
example_3: I don't like her attitude toward older people.
example_3_translation: Não gosto da atitude dela em relação às pessoas mais velhas.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: AT-ti-tude. Emphasize first: AT-ti-tude
memory_hook: At-ti-tude - your 'titude' (state) of being - how you act

primary_meaning: a particular way of thinking about something
secondary_meaning: the ability to see things clearly
usage_notes: Use with 'from my perspective', 'different perspective', 'gain perspective'
common_collocations: different perspective, from my perspective, new perspective, gain perspective
image_search_query: artist perspective or multiple viewpoints from different angles
image_context: An artist looking at a landscape from different angles or multiple arrows showing different perspectives
example_1: Try to see it from my perspective.
example_1_translation: Tente ver da minha perspectiva.
example_2: That gave me a different perspective on the problem.
example_2_translation: Isso me deu uma perspectiva diferente sobre o problema.
example_3: Writing in a journal helps me gain perspective.
example_3_translation: Escrever em um diário me ajuda a ganhar perspectiva.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: per-SPEC-tive. Emphasize second: per-SPEC-tive
memory_hook: Per-spec-tive - 'spec' (look) through - how you look at things

primary_meaning: a feeling that something is true or real
secondary_meaning: a strong opinion about something
usage_notes: Use with 'belief in', 'strong belief', 'religious beliefs'
common_collocations: belief in, strong belief, religious belief, personal belief
image_search_query: person praying or scales balancing beliefs
image_context: Hands clasped in prayer or scales balancing different belief systems
example_1: Her belief in hard work helped her succeed.
example_1_translation: A crença dela no trabalho duro a ajudou a ter sucesso.
example_2: Everyone has the right to their own beliefs.
example_2_translation: Todos têm o direito às suas próprias crenças.
example_3: That goes against my religious beliefs.
example_3_translation: Isso vai contra minhas crenças religiosas.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: be-LIEF. Rhymes with 'leaf' and 'chief'
memory_hook: Be-lief - be-'lieve' - what you believe is true

primary_meaning: a moral rule or belief that guides behavior
secondary_meaning: a basic truth or law
usage_notes: Use with 'on principle', 'basic principles', 'stand on principle'
common_collocations: on principle, basic principles, moral principles, stand on principle
image_search_query: scales of justice or person standing firm on principles
image_context: Scales of justice or a person standing firmly with arms crossed representing strong principles
example_1: She refused on principle to accept the offer.
example_1_translation: Ela recusou por princípio não aceitar a oferta.
example_2: It's a matter of principle for me.
example_2_translation: É uma questão de princípio para mim.
example_3: The basic principles of democracy are important.
example_3_translation: Os princípios básicos da democracia são importantes.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: PRIN-ci-ple. Emphasize first: PRIN-ci-ple
memory_hook: Prin-ci-ple - 'prin' (prime) 'ciple' (rule) - basic rules

primary_meaning: who a person is or what a group represents
secondary_meaning: the qualities that make someone who they are
usage_notes: Use with 'sense of identity', 'cultural identity', 'personal identity'
common_collocations: sense of identity, cultural identity, personal identity, national identity
image_search_query: person silhouette or ID card representing identity
image_context: A person's silhouette against a colorful background or an ID card showing identity documents
example_1: Losing his job affected his sense of identity.
example_1_translation: Perder o trabalho afetou sua identidade.
example_2: The city has a strong cultural identity.
example_2_translation: A cidade tem uma forte identidade cultural.
example_3: She explores her identity through her art.
example_3_translation: Ela explora sua identidade através da arte.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: i-DEN-ti-ty. Emphasize second: i-DEN-ti-ty
memory_hook: I-den-ti-ty - 'den' (denote) what denotes you - who you are

primary_meaning: the customs and beliefs of a particular group
secondary_meaning: art, music, and literature
usage_notes: Use with 'culture shock', 'popular culture', 'work culture'
common_collocations: popular culture, culture shock, work culture, foreign culture
image_search_query: diverse cultural symbols or traditional celebration
image_context: Colorful flags and symbols from different cultures or people celebrating traditional festivals
example_1: The company has a very positive work culture.
example_1_translation: A empresa tem uma cultura de trabalho muito positiva.
example_2: I want to learn more about different cultures.
example_2_translation: Quero aprender mais sobre diferentes culturas.
example_3: She experienced culture shock when she moved abroad.
example_3_translation: Ela experimentou choque cultural quando se mudou para o exterior.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: CUL-ture. Emphasize first: CUL-ture
memory_hook: Cul-ture - cultivate your mind with knowledge

primary_meaning: a belief or custom passed from one generation to another
secondary_meaning: the practice of following traditional ways
usage_notes: Use with 'by tradition', 'family tradition', 'break with tradition'
common_collocations: family tradition, by tradition, break tradition, cultural tradition
image_search_query: family gathering or cultural ceremony representing tradition
image_context: Families gathering for a holiday dinner or a cultural ceremony showing traditional practices
example_1: It's a family tradition to visit relatives at Christmas.
example_1_translation: É uma tradição familiar visitar parentes no Natal.
example_2: By tradition, the eldest son inherits the business.
example_2_translation: Por tradição, o filho mais velho herda o negócio.
example_3: We broke with tradition and ate in a restaurant instead.
example_3_translation: Quebramos a tradição e comemos em um restaurante.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: tra-DI-tion. Emphasize second: tra-DI-tion
memory_hook: Tra-di-tion - from 'tradere' (to hand down) - passed down

primary_meaning: belief in a god or gods
secondary_meaning: a particular system of faith and worship
usage_notes: Use with 'freedom of religion', 'practice religion', 'different religions'
common_collocations: freedom of religion, practice religion, world religions, religious beliefs
image_search_query: church temple mosque or religious symbols
image_context: Various religious buildings or symbols from Christianity, Islam, Judaism, and other religions
example_1: Freedom of religion is a basic human right.
example_1_translation: Liberdade de religião é um direito humano básico.
example_2: There are many different religions in the world.
example_2_translation: Existem muitas religiões diferentes no mundo.
example_3: She practices her religion every day.
example_3_translation: Ela pratica sua religião todos os dias.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: re-LI-gion. Emphasize second: re-LI-gion
memory_hook: Re-li-gion - re-'lig' (bind) - binding to something greater

primary_meaning: strong belief in something without proof
secondary_meaning: trust in someone or something
usage_notes: Use with 'have faith', 'lose faith', 'faith in'
common_collocations: have faith, lose faith, faith in, keep the faith
image_search_query: person praying or hands held in trust
image_context: Hands clasped in prayer or two people shaking hands showing trust and faith
example_1: I have faith in your abilities.
example_1_translation: Tenho fé nas suas habilidades.
example_2: She never lost faith, even in difficult times.
example_2_translation: Ela nunca perdeu a fé, mesmo em tempos difíceis.
example_3: His faith gives him strength.
example_3_translation: Sua fé lhe dá força.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: FAITH. Rhymes with 'space' and 'face'
memory_hook: Faith - sounds like 'fate' - destiny and belief
common_mistakes: 'Faith' not 'fath' - spelled with 'ith' at the end

primary_meaning: moral rules that govern behavior
secondary_meaning: the study of what is right and wrong
usage_notes: Use with 'business ethics', 'medical ethics', 'personal ethics'
common_collocations: business ethics, medical ethics, personal ethics, code of ethics
image_search_query: scales of justice or ethical decision making
image_context: Scales of justice or a person at a crossroads making ethical decisions
example_1: The company has strict ethics guidelines.
example_1_translation: A empresa tem diretrizes éticas rigorosas.
example_2: Medical ethics don't allow this procedure.
example_2_translation: A ética médica não permite este procedimento.
example_3: His personal ethics wouldn't let him lie.
example_3_translation: Seus princípios pessoais não o deixariam mentir.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: ETH-ics. Emphasize first: ETH-ics
memory_hook: Eth-ics - 'eth' (custom) - customs of right and wrong
common_mistakes: 'Ethics' is plural - 'ethics are important' not 'ethics is'

primary_meaning: what people think of someone or something
secondary_meaning: the opinion that is generally held about someone
usage_notes: Use with 'good reputation', 'damage reputation', ' reputation for'
common_collocations: good reputation, damage reputation, reputation for, build reputation
image_search_query: star rating or person with thumbs up representing reputation
image_context: Five stars or a person with a golden badge representing a good reputation
example_1: This restaurant has a great reputation.
example_1_translation: Este restaurante tem uma ótima reputação.
example_2: The scandal damaged his reputation.
example_2_translation: O escândalo prejudicou sua reputação.
example_3: She built her reputation through hard work.
example_3_translation: Ela construiu sua reputação através do trabalho duro.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: rep-u-TA-tion. Emphasize third: rep-u-TA-tion
memory_hook: Rep-u-ta-tion - re-putation - what you 'put' out about yourself

primary_meaning: the power to affect someone's behavior or decisions
secondary_meaning: the effect that something has on something else
usage_notes: Use with 'have influence', 'under the influence', 'influence on'
common_collocations: have influence, influence on, big influence, influence over
image_search_query: magnet attracting objects or person leading others
image_context: A magnet attracting metal objects or a leader influencing a group of people
example_1: She has a lot of influence over the team.
example_1_translation: Ela tem muita influência sobre a equipe.
example_2: Social media influences young people greatly.
example_2_translation: As redes sociais influenciam muito os jovens.
example_3: His greatest influence was his teacher.
example_3_translation: Sua maior influência foi seu professor.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: IN-flu-ence. Emphasize first: IN-flu-ence
memory_hook: In-flu-ence - 'flu' (flow) in - flowing into someone

primary_meaning: a powerful effect that something has on something
secondary_meaning: the action of one thing hitting another
usage_notes: Use with 'impact on', 'have an impact', 'major impact'
common_collocations: impact on, have an impact, major impact, environmental impact
image_search_query: meteor hitting earth or ripple effect in water
image_context: A meteor striking earth or a stone creating ripples in water showing impact
example_1: The new policy will have a major impact on the economy.
example_1_translation: A nova política terá um grande impacto na economia.
example_2: We felt the impact of the recession.
example_2_translation: Sentimos o impacto da recessão.
example_3: Technology has changed our lives for better and worse.
example_3_translation: A tecnologia mudou nossas vidas para melhor e para pior.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: IM-pact. Emphasize first: IM-pact
memory_hook: Im-pact - 'im' (into) 'pact' (packed) - packed into something
common_mistakes: 'Impact' can be noun or verb but pronunciation differs: IM-pact (noun) vs im-PACT (verb)

primary_meaning: a result of a particular action or situation
secondary_meaning: the negative results of an action
usage_notes: Use with 'as a consequence', 'face consequences', 'serious consequences'
common_collocations: face consequences, serious consequences, as a consequence, consequences of
image_search_query: dominoes falling or cause and effect diagram
image_context: Dominoes falling in a line or a flowchart showing cause and consequence
example_1: You must face the consequences of your actions.
example_1_translation: Você deve enfrentar as consequências de suas ações.
example_2: As a consequence, we had to cancel the project.
example_2_translation: Como consequência, tivemos que cancelar o projeto.
example_3: The consequences of climate change are serious.
example_3_translation: As consequências das mudanças climáticas são graves.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: CON-se-quence. Emphasize first: CON-se-quence
memory_hook: Con-se-quence - 'con' (with) 'sequence' - what follows after

primary_meaning: the importance of something
secondary_meaning: the meaning of something
usage_notes: Use with 'of significance', 'historical significance', 'no significance'
common_collocations: of significance, historical significance, no significance, statistical significance
image_search_query: important symbol or milestone marker
image_context: A golden star or a milestone marker showing significance of an event
example_1: The discovery was of great historical significance.
example_1_translation: A descoberta foi de grande significado histórico.
example_2: She didn't understand the significance of his words.
example_2_translation: Ela não entendeu o significado das palavras dele.
example_3: The test showed statistical significance.
example_3_translation: O teste mostrou significância estatística.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: sig-NIF-i-cance. Emphasize second: sig-NIF-i-cance
memory_hook: Sig-nif-i-cance - 'sign' 'ify' 'cance' - what signifies something

primary_meaning: something that is more important than other things
secondary_meaning: the right to come before others
usage_notes: Use with 'top priority', 'set priorities', 'make something a priority'
common_collocations: top priority, set priorities, first priority, make it a priority
image_search_query: numbered list or person checking important tasks
image_context: A to-do list with numbers showing priorities or a person checking important items first
example_1: Health is my top priority right now.
example_1_translation: A saúde é minha principal prioridade agora.
example_2: You need to set your priorities.
example_2_translation: Você precisa definir suas prioridades.
example_3: Let's focus on the priority tasks first.
example_3_translation: Vamos focar nas tarefas prioritárias primeiro.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: pri-OR-i-ty. Emphasize second: pri-OR-i-ty
memory_hook: Pri-or-i-ty - 'prior' (before) - what comes before
common_mistakes: 'Priority' not 'priorities' when singular - 'a priority' not 'a priorities'

primary_meaning: something that helps you succeed or be better
secondary_meaning: a good feature of something
usage_notes: Use with 'take advantage of', 'have an advantage', 'advantage over'
common_collocations: have an advantage, take advantage of, big advantage, advantage over
image_search_query: winner podium or chess piece having advantage
image_context: A winner on a podium or a chess game showing one piece with advantage
example_1: Having a car gives you an advantage in this city.
example_1_translation: Ter um carro dá a você uma vantagem nesta cidade.
example_2: We should take advantage of this opportunity.
example_2_translation: Devemos aproveitar esta oportunidade.
example_3: The main advantage of living here is the weather.
example_3_translation: A principal vantagem de viver aqui é o clima.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: ad-VAN-tage. Emphasize second: ad-VAN-tage
memory_hook: Ad-van-tage - 'ad' (to) 'vantage' (view) - a view ahead

primary_meaning: something that makes you less successful
secondary_meaning: a problem or bad feature
usage_notes: Use with 'disadvantage of', 'at a disadvantage', 'disadvantages include'
common_collocations: at a disadvantage, disadvantage of, major disadvantage, disadvantages include
image_search_query: person with obstacle or scales with disadvantage
image_context: A person carrying heavy weights or scales showing disadvantage
example_1: Not speaking English is a major disadvantage.
example_1_translation: Não falar inglês é uma grande desvantagem.
example_2: She was at a disadvantage compared to the other candidates.
example_2_translation: Ela estava em desvantagem em comparação com os outros candidatos.
example_3: The main disadvantage of the plan is the cost.
example_3_translation: A principal desvantagem do plano é o custo.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: dis-ad-VAN-tage. Emphasize third: dis-ad-VAN-tage
memory_hook: Dis-advantage - 'dis' (opposite) 'advantage' - opposite of advantage
common_mistakes: 'Disadvantage' is one word, not two: 'dis advantage'

primary_meaning: something that blocks your way or makes it difficult
secondary_meaning: a problem that prevents you from doing something
usage_notes: Use with 'obstacle to', 'overcome obstacles', 'major obstacle'
common_collocations: overcome obstacles, major obstacle, obstacle to, obstacle course
image_search_query: roadblock or person jumping over hurdle
image_context: A roadblock or barrier on a road, or an athlete jumping over hurdles
example_1: The main obstacle to success is fear.
example_1_translation: O principal obstáculo para o sucesso é o medo.
example_2: We overcame many obstacles to reach our goal.
example_2_translation: Superamos muitos obstáculos para alcançar nosso objetivo.
example_3: Lack of money isn't an obstacle if you're determined.
example_3_translation: Falta de dinheiro não é um obstáculo se você estiver determinado.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: OB-sta-cle. Emphasize first: OB-sta-cle
memory_hook: Ob-sta-cle - 'ob' (against) 'stacle' (stand) - standing against you

primary_meaning: a chance to do something
secondary_meaning: a favorable time or situation
usage_notes: Use with 'opportunity to', 'job opportunity', 'once in a lifetime opportunity'
common_collocations: opportunity to, job opportunity, career opportunity, once in a lifetime opportunity
image_search_query: open door or golden key representing opportunity
image_context: An open door with light coming through or a golden key representing opportunity
example_1: This is a great opportunity to learn new skills.
example_1_translation: Esta é uma ótima oportunidade de aprender novas habilidades.
example_2: There are many job opportunities in this city.
example_2_translation: Há muitas oportunidades de emprego nesta cidade.
example_3: Don't miss this opportunity - it may not come again.
example_3_translation: Não perca esta oportunidade - pode não acontecer de novo.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: op-por-TU-ni-ty. Emphasize third: op-por-TU-ni-ty
memory_hook: Op-por-tu-ni-ty - 'oppor-tune' - when the time is right

primary_meaning: a warning that something bad might happen
secondary_meaning: something that could cause danger or damage
usage_notes: Use with 'threat to', 'pose a threat', 'death threats'
common_collocations: pose a threat, threat to, under threat, security threat
image_search_query: storm clouds or warning sign representing threat
image_context: Dark storm clouds gathering or a warning sign indicating danger ahead
example_1: Climate change is a serious threat to our planet.
example_1_translation: A mudança climática é uma séria ameaça ao nosso planeta.
example_2: He received threats after winning the case.
example_2_translation: Ele recebeu ameaças depois de vencer o caso.
example_3: The company is under threat of closure.
example_3_translation: A empresa está sob ameaça de fechamento.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: THREAT. Rhymes with 'get' and 'set'
memory_hook: Threat - sounds like 'threaten' - a warning
